channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: FULL LIST: 16 countries chasing WAFCON 2026 glory

item.description:
Discover the 16 countries battling for WAFCON 2026 glory and World Cup spots. See rankings, favourites like Nigeria, and dark horses in this comprehensive

item.title: Guardiola turns down Italy job, prioritises family time — Report
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Pep Guardiola has rejected the Italy national team coaching job, choosing to prioritise family time. Find out why Guardiola turned down the offer.
Read More: https://punchng.com/guardiola-turns-down-italy-job-prioritises-family-time-report/

item.title: VIDEO: Uzodimma can’t speak for everybody, Otti clears air on 2027 South-East backing of Tinubu
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Abia State Governor Alex Otti distances himself from Uzodimma’s claim of South-East governors backing Tinubu for 2027, stating Uzodimma can’t speak for all
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-uzodimma-cant-speak-for-everybody-otti-clears-air-on-2027-south-east-backing-of-tinubu/

item.title: 'Like a movie playing in my head' | Klopp honoured to take Germany job
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Jurgen Klopp admits he is still coming to terms with being confirmed as the new head coach of the German national team, describing it as 'like a movie in my head'.

item.title: NSCDC arrests 10 students over banned ‘signed out’ celebration in Adamawa
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
NSCDC arrests 10 students in Adamawa for participating in the banned ‘signed out’ celebration, which caused public nuisance and threatened order.

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Governor Adeleke unveils the first phase of the 3.5km Ila dual carriageway in Osun State, promising enhanced commerce, mobility, and safety for residents.
Read More: https://punchng.com/adeleke-unveils-first-phase-of-3-5km-ila-dual-carriageway/

item.title: Elizabeth Agboola: Six Destinations Nigerians Are Now Falling in Love With
item.dc:creator: BellaNaija
item.description: Before, if you asked a Nigerian where they wanted to travel, the answers were almost predictable: London, Dubai, New York, or perhaps South Africa. However, today, Nigerians are now searching for better value, fresh experiences and destinations that offer something beyond the usual holiday favourite

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Tech
item.description: Artificial intelligence could become one of the biggest drivers of Nigeria’s next phase of economic growth and investment, according to

item.title: Could Adeyemi's Barcelona move see Flick unlock winger's true potential?
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Perhaps Karim Adeyemi's move to Barcelona should not come as a surprise even if usurping Lamine Yamal as the team's star left-footed right-winger is impossible. After all, it was Hansi Flick, Barca’s head coach, who gave Adeyemi his debut for Germany.

item.title: Dangote Refinery invites private investors in $2.5bn pre-IPO landmark
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: The record-breaking primary placement brings external capital into the Nigerian mega-complex for the first time, paving the way for a blockbuster public listing later this year.

item.title: The Weekend Watchlist | These Are Films/Series To Watch This Weekend
item.dc:creator: YNaija
item.description: The weekend is underway, and in true Ynaija fashion, we are committed to helping you have the most relaxing time ever. With a blend of action thrillers, comedy, drama, and adventure, we’ve done the work, compiled the movies, and given you a list that will keep your weekend fun, entertaining, and eng

item.title: JRB: 16 states adopt harmonised tax framework to curb multiple taxation
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: The Joint Revenue Board (JRB) has said that 16 out of the 36 Nigerian states have now adopted a harmonised Taxes and Levies framework aimed at eliminating multiple taxation and creating a more predictable business environment. The post JRB: 16 states adopt harmonised tax framework to curb multiple t

item.title: 68 LawyersTo Be Conferred With SAN Rank
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Legal Practitioners’ Privileges Committee (LPPC) has released the names of 68 lawyers to be conferred with the rank of Senior Advocate of Nigeria (SAN). LPPC’s secretary and chief registrar of the Supreme Court, Kabir Akanbi, disclosed this in a statement yesterday. Akanbi said the LPPC to

item.title: Energy expert urges commercial review of $27 billion African Atlantic Gas Pipeline
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Mr Kunle said Nigeria must prioritise domestic gas utilisation over ambitious regional export projects until critical questions about gas supply, financing and market demand are resolved. The post Energy expert urges commercial review of $27 billion African Atlantic Gas Pipeline appeared first on Pr

item.title: Residents Decry Incessant Accidents, Trailer Gridlocks On Kwara Road
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Residents and commuters in Ilorin, the Kwara State capital, have called on the state government to address the persistent road accidents and traffic congestion on Asa-Dam Road. The residents described the incessant auto crashes on the road as alarming. They attributed most of the accidents on the ro

item.title: China Condemns Fresh US Tariffs, Warns Washington Against Trade War
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: China has condemned the latest tariffs imposed by the United States on its exports and those of 59 other countries over alleged forced labour concerns, warning that renewed trade restrictions could reignite global trade tensions. The new US tariffs, announced on Friday, range from 10 to 12.5 per cen

item.title: SERAP Opposes Bill Requiring Social Media Platforms To Establish Offices In Nigeria
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Socio-Economic Rights and Accountability Project (SERAP) has criticised a proposed amendment to the Nigeria Data Protection Act, warning that it could restrict civic space, freedom of expression and access to information. item.title: Tens of thousands evacuated from French tourist spot as wildfires spread
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The fire has raged in the region for several days with officials now ordering the evacuation of the entire Cap Ferret peninsula.

item.description: Nigerian students are passionately hunting for scholarships in Rwanda due to its reputation for high-quality, uninterrupted education, and accessible premier institutions like the CMU-Africa and ALU. item.title: Gombe Student Found Dead In Stagnant Water
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The body of a 200-level student of the Federal University of Kashere (FUK), Gombe State, has been recovered from a stagnant water in Sabon Garin Yero Village, near Kashere town, Akko local government area. The deceased was identified as Enoch Sunday Maisamari, a 28-year-old student of the Department

item.title: Super Falcons begin WAFCON title defence against Malawi on Sunday
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria’s Super Falcons will begin their campaign at the 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (WAFCON) against Malawi on Sunday in Morocco as they seek a record-extending 11th continental title. The tournament, originally scheduled for March, will hold from July 26 to Aug. 16, with 16 teams competing

item.title: Klopp starts Germany reign by threatening to quit
item.dc:creator: BBC Sport Football
item.description: Jurgen Klopp starts his reign as Germany's head coach by threatening to quit if the media invades his family's privacy.

item.description: The 'Farmgate' scandal nearly cost President Cyril Ramaphosa the leadership of his African National Congress in 2022.

item.title: Court suspends South African President Ramaphosa impeachment over $580,000 scandal

item.description: The review application is expected to be heard in September, meaning the impeachment process will remain on hold until the court reaches a decision.

item.dc:creator: TVC News

item.description: Chess in Slums Africa founder, Tunde Onakoya, has partnered with Jobs59 founder, Sam Otigba, and AltSchool Africa founder, HackSultan, to launch a ₦250 million scholarship programme aimed at equipping National Youth Service Corps (NYSC) members and recent graduates with in-demand technology skills.

item.description: Germany on Friday appointed Jurgen Klopp as its new head coach of the national football team. item.title: Here’s how much revenue S&P 500 companies make from overseas
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The U.S. stock market really is domestic — barely over a quarter of revenue from America’s top 500 companies comes from abroad.

item.title: Dollar Set for Best Week in a Month as Haven Flows Offer Support
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: The dollar is heading for its best week in a month as investors return to the traditional haven amid heightened geopolitical tensions.

item.title: Eleven poets battle for $100,000 Nigeria Literature Prize glory
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The race for the coveted $100,000 Nigeria Prize for Literature has intensified with the unveiling of an 11-title longlist for the 2026 edition dedicated to poetry, following a rigorous selection process from 223 entries submitted by poets across the country and beyond. The post Eleven poets battle f

item.title: Airtel Africa Picks London for Mobile Money IPO, Nigeria Remains its Fastest-Growing Bet
item.dc:creator: TechEconomy
item.description: Airtel Africa has named London as its preferred listing (IPO) venue for Airtel Money, the mobile financial services unit that processed more than $245 billion in transactions on an annualised basis in the June quarter. The Group’s announcement in the first-quarter results published Thursday sh

item.title: Gulf States Set for Debt Binge to Build Routes Avoiding Hormuz
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Traders are preparing for a spike in borrowing from Persian Gulf nations as the region’s oil exporters seek financing to build costly bypasses around the Strait of Hormuz.

item.title: UK dependent visa ban: Why more Nigerians are exploring the innovator founder route
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Changes to the United Kingdom's immigration rules have made it more difficult for many migrants to relocate with their families. The post UK dependent visa ban: Why more Nigerians are exploring the innovator founder route appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: 2027: INEC can’t be truly independent without financial autonomy — Amupitan
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Chairman of the Independent National Electoral Commission, INEC, Professor Joash Amupitan, says the commission cannot be truly independent without financial autonomy. Amupitan made this assertion on Thursday at the public presentation of ‘Shadows: Protest Essays on Africa’s Most Conseq

item.title: As geopolitical tension spikes, these market veterans say a classic stock-market buying opportunity has arrived
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: The S&P 500 has moved below its 50-day moving average, presenting a strong buying opportunity, according to Yardeni Research and Fundstrat.

item.title: One dead, two injured as truck overturns on Kara bridge in Lagos
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: A man has lost his life while two others sustained injuries after a truck overturned on Kara Bridge, inward Mowe-Ibafo, in the early hours of Friday, causing a multiple-vehicle crash and severe traffic disruption. The Lagos State Emergency Management Agency, LASEMA, disclosed this in a statemen

item.title: Phillips Consulting launches the 2026 State Performance Index (pSPI)
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The index provides benchmark for measuring governance, performance and development across Nigeria's 36 states and the Federal Capital Territory The post Phillips Consulting launches the 2026 State Performance Index (pSPI) appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: China opposes new US tariffs, warns against trade wars
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: The new tariffs replace an expiring global duty rolled out by President Donald Trump earlier this year. The post China opposes new US tariffs, warns against trade wars appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Abia CSOs Dialogue, Share Ideas On How To Serve Society Better
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Over 20 civil society organisations (non-profit organisations) operating in Abia State met in Aba, the commercial hub of the state, to dialogue on building strategic partnerships for sustainable development and serving society better. The gathering, organised by the Centre for Development Practice (

item.title: SGF Urges Community Pharmacists To Partner NAFDAC Against Fake Drugs
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Secretary to the Government of the Federation (SGF), Senator George Akume, has called on the Association of Community Pharmacists of Nigeria (ACPN) to strengthen collaboration with the National Agency for Food and Drug Administration and Control (NAFDAC) to combat the spread of counterfeit and subst
